patterns.slot-areas-customization: Kanban provides three customization slots: 'title-bar-actions' (lines 59-82) replaces default title bar icons (expand, collapse, help, minimize, window, close), 'action-bar' (lines 91-127) replaces default action buttons and filters, and 'action-dropdown' for dropdown controls. When slots are not provided, default UI elements are rendered. Title bar actions include 6 icon buttons, action bar includes delete, group, customize, and view controls.
patterns.column-and-card-structure: Kanban uses KanbanColumn array (id, title, cards[]) where each column contains KanbanCardProps array. Columns are wrapped in Card components (line 145) and rendered in a horizontal scrollable layout (kanban__columns-wrapper, lines 133-221). Column title determines border color via conditional classes: titles containing 'in progress' get kanban__column-card--in-progress, 'done' gets kanban__column-card--done (lines 136-142).
patterns.action-buttons-vs-dropdowns: The action bar supports two prop patterns: actionButtons array with {text, icon} for Button components (lines 93-105), or actionDropdowns array with {text, options, value} for Dropdown components. When neither is provided, default actions are rendered (lines 108-127). Use actionButtons for simple click actions, actionDropdowns for selection-based filters or view changes.

events.name: click
events.description: Clicks on action buttons, dropdowns, or cards; implement drag/drop in parent if needed

state.description: Column and card data passed as props; reorder/state managed by parent.
